channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by AI

item.title: Taiwan Denies Knowledge of US Arms Pause Despite Navy Secretary Statement
item.description: Taiwan presidential office says it has not received any notification of changes to military sales after Acting Navy Secretary Hung Cao said the US was pausing a $14 billion arms package to ensure munitions for the Iran operation.

item.title: Netanyahu Leaked Secret UAE Wartime Visit to Boost Domestic Standing
item.description: Analysis reveals Israeli PM Netanyahu deliberately disclosed his clandestine March meeting with UAE President MBZ to portray himself as a regional statesman ahead of elections, drawing Emirati anger and potentially undermining Gulf balancing act.

item.title: Injured Palestinian Girls Arrive in Boston for Medical Treatment
item.description: Three Palestinian children from Gaza, including a double amputee and burn victims, arrived in the US for urgent medical care as part of HEAL Palestine evacuation efforts, highlighting Gaza healthcare collapse.

item.title: UK Refuses to Revoke Hong Kong Trade Office Privileges After Spy Case
item.description: British government says Hong Kong Economic and Trade Office can continue legitimate activity despite conviction of two men linked to the office for spying on activists, resisting political pressure to revoke diplomatic privileges.

item.title: Pakistan mediates US-Iran peace talks as uranium dispute persists
item.description: Iran's foreign minister met his Pakistani counterpart to discuss proposals to end the US-Israeli war. Tehran and Washington remain at odds over uranium stockpile and Strait of Hormuz controls.

item.title: Quad foreign ministers to meet in Delhi amid China tensions
item.description: The US, Japan, Australia and India will hold a meeting of Quad foreign ministers in New Delhi on May 26, Japan's foreign ministry announced.

item.title: Turkish court ousts main opposition leader Ozel
item.description: A Turkish court annulled the 2023 party congress that elected opposition CHP leader Ozgur Ozel, effectively removing him from his position and dealing a blow to President Erdogan's challengers.

item.title: China's Hengli petrochemical empire faces US sanctions chill
item.description: The Hengli Group, one of China's largest private oil refiners, has been thrust into the center of a geopolitical power struggle as US sanctions target the company.

item.title: APEC trade envoys gather in China to discuss supply chain resilience
item.description: Asia-Pacific trade envoys met in China to discuss multilateral cooperation and supply chain resilience amid global shocks including the US-Iranian conflict.

item.title: Trump announces additional 5,000 US troops for Poland
item.description: President Trump declared the US will send 5,000 additional troops to Poland, reversing recent administration moves to reduce European deployments. The announcement stirred confusion amid contradictory statements about US troop presence in Europe.

item.title: Belarus will not be dragged into Ukraine war, Lukashenko says
item.description: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko dismissed any notion Belarus would join Russia in the Ukraine war, but stated Belarus and Russia would jointly defend themselves if attacked. The statement comes amid ongoing regional tensions.

item.title: Latvia issues drone alert near Belarus border
item.description: Latvia's defense ministry issued a drone alert after unidentified aerial activity was detected near the Belarus border, raising concerns about regional security.

item.title: US House Republicans cancel Iran war powers vote
item.description: House Republicans cancelled a planned vote on limiting presidential war powers regarding Iran, amid ongoing US military operations in the region.

item.title: US sanctions Hezbollah-aligned individuals in Lebanon
item.description: The United States imposed sanctions on nine Hezbollah-aligned individuals in Lebanon, as Washington continues pressure on the Iran-backed group.

item.title: White House postpones Trump AI signing ceremony
item.description: The White House postponed President Trump's AI signing ceremony, Axios reported, amid ongoing policy discussions.

item.title: US approves potential $1.081B HAWK missile system sale to Ukraine
item.description: The United States approved a potential $1.081 billion sale of HAWK missile systems to Ukraine, continuing military support for Kyiv's defense against Russian aggression.

item.title: Turkish opposition leader vows to stay after court ousts him
item.description: Turkish opposition leader Ozgur Ozel vowed to remain in politics after a court ruling annulled his election as CHP chairman, vowing to continue his political fight.

item.title: Israel deports all foreign activists from Gaza flotilla
item.description: Israel deported all foreign activists who were aboard the Gaza-bound aid flotilla that was seized by Israeli forces, following international pressure over the incident.

item.title: Czech President: Ukraine is Europe's defense laboratory
item.description: Czech President Petr Pavel stated that Ukraine is not a charity case but rather Europe's defense laboratory, emphasizing the strategic importance of continued support for Ukrainian defense capabilities.

item.title: Iran's Supreme Leader says enriched uranium must stay in Iran
item.description: Iranian sources report that Supreme Leader Khamenei has insisted that enriched uranium must remain in Iran, a key sticking point in negotiations with the United States.

item.title: South Korea's Lee Jae-myung claims victory in presidential election
item.description: Opposition candidate Lee Jae-myung declared victory in South Korea's presidential election, signaling a potential shift in the country's domestic and foreign policy direction.

item.title: US-Iran conflict dominates Trump-Putin press conference in Beijing
item.description: President Trump and President Putin held a joint press conference in Beijing following the Xi summit, addressing the ongoing US-Iran military conflict and potential diplomatic resolutions.

item.title: China, Russia, US leaders hold historic Beijing summit
item.description: The leaders of China, Russia, and the United States convened in Beijing for a historic three-way summit, the first such meeting in decades, addressing global security challenges including the Iran crisis.

item.title: European leaders convene emergency meeting on Iran crisis
item.description: European leaders gathered for an emergency meeting to discuss the escalating Iran crisis and coordinate their response to the military conflict between the US and Iran.
